items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked
Vehicle government registration card (ISTIMARA) and Government periodic inspection are maintained and valid at all times for equipment that has a vehicle plate.
Durable load chart is provided in an accessible location
Work areas chart is available
Operator is certified (for the inspected crane type/model) and licensed
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
Rated capacity of crane is marked (Arabic & English)
Fire extinguisher is in place with minimum rating 10 BC
Markings and direction of all control levers are clearly displayed
Crane backward stability is in accordance with manufacturers standard (minimum radius and no load condition )
Load- holding check valve for boom is operational
Two (2) full wraps of rope remain on the drum when hook is in the extreme low position
Swing control is smoothly operating on acceleration and deceleration
Swing braking operates, as well as positive locking devices (manual and auto)
Travel controls are properly operating/functioning
Auxiliary travel controls function correctly for multiple control- station cranes
Travel brakes and locks for crawler cranes are correctly operating
Travel brakes and locks for wheel-mounted cranes are correctly operating
Power plant controls of superstructure -mounted power plant working properly
Rotation-resistant and fiber-core ropes are not used for boom hoist reeving
Sheave grooves have surface smoothness without any defects
Sheave guards are in place on the lower block
All sheaves can be lubricated (except permanently lubricated types)
All cab glazing are safety glazing material
Verify cab doors functionality and condition
Seat belt is fitted and in good condition
Walking platforms and areas of structure are skid-resistance
Guard rails are fitted
Fixed or telescoping bumper for boom stop is functional (if fitted)
Shock absorbing bumper for boom stop is functional (if fitted)
Hydraulic boom elevating cylinder, used as boom stop, is functional (if fitted)
Boom angle indicator is fitted, operable, and can be read from the operator station
Boom shut-off or hydraulic reliefs provided for high angle boom condition
Boom length indicator is fitted for telescoping booms ,and is working
Anti-two-blocking devices are fitted and working (main and auxiliary)
Operation and accuracy of the fitted Load Indicator, RCI or RCL (compare with load chart)
LMI is fitted, operational and accurate for bool length, rated load, angle, radius, etc. (for 3 ton cranes or more)
Outriggers and jacks are not leaking oil
Jack is in good operating condition at each outrigger
items.referenceStandard: ASME B30.5 sec: ASME B30.5 Sec:5-2.1.3 (d)

items.acceptanceCriteria: Each outrigger jack locking device is functional (if fitted)
No leakage from each jack seal when the crane is jacked up
Outrigger jack bolts are fully secure
All road wheel nuts are fully secure
All tires are serviceable and without damage or excess wear
Fuel tank supports and straps are secure
Hydraulic oil tank supports and straps are secure
Underside of chassis shows no obvious faults
Outrigger boxes are not cracked
Front and rear steering cylinder anchor points and pins are secure
Track rod end joints are not worn and are secure
Axle bolts and nuts are fully secure
Wheel hub oil seals are not leaking
Brake linings are not oil contaminated
Brake hoses have not deteriorated, e.g. cracks, splits, leaks, etc.
All chassis cross members are not cracked or corroded
Transmission mounting bolts and bushings are secure
All drive shaft bolts and universal joints are not worn
Ring gear bolts are fully secure
All hydraulic hoses under chassis have not deteriorated (cracks , splits , leaks )
Engine shows no major oil leakage
Radiator does not leak water

Rope is free of kinking, crushing, birdcaging, or any other damage resulting in distortion of the rope structure • No more 1 broken wire protruding from the core • There are no reductions from nominal rope diameter of more than 5% For running ropes: • No more than 6 randomly broken wires in 1 lay or 3 broken wires in 1 strand of 1 lay For rotation resistance ropes :• No more than 2 randomly distributed broken wires in six rope diameters or 4 randomly distributed broken wires in 30 rope diameters • Evidence of any heat damage from any cause.
Main hoist drum mounting bolts are secure
Rear telescope side and top wear pads are serviceable
Bottom and side wear pads of telescope sections are serviceable
Boom heel pin bushes are not worn (rock boom up and down to check this)
Center swivel coupling pipes and hoses do not leak
Center swivel coupling bolts are all secure
Boom lift cylinder anchor bolts and bushings are secure
Swing gearbox bottom oil seal and drive gear are in a serviceable condition (Should be well lubricated)
Wire rope clips shall be drop-forged steel of the single saddle U bolt or doubled saddle type clip for end termination.
Emergency switch / Power take off switch need to be verified
Horn and wind speed anemometer are working
Crane level indicator is fitted and working
Engine gauges and associated indicators are working
The warning sign stating power line required clearances is visible to the operator
Load block sheaves turn freely and are undamaged
Dead end rope anchor is correctly made with a suitable wedge socket and loop-back termination
Hook assemblies are equipped with safety latches
Hook assembly labeling and manufacturer data is clearly marked
Hook's weight is clearly marked/printed on the hook
Safe working load of hook is clearly marked on the item
Hook does not show defects such as nicks, cracks and gouges
Hook is not bent or twisted
Hook is not distorted in the throat opening • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1∕4 in. (6 mm) compared to new hook, or as per manufacturer recommendations
Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks
Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook)
Inoperative latch (if provided) — any damaged latch or malfunctioning latch that does not close the hook’s throat
Rope guards and locked in position with pins (hook block)
Dead end rope anchor is fitted with a lock pin (hook block)
Dead end rope anchor is correctly made with a suitable wedge socket and loop- back termination
Reverse travel alarm is operational.
Turning signals and indicators are working correctly
Road lighting, including spotlights, are in good condition and operable
Dry friction brakes and clutches shall be protected against rain and other liquids such as oil and lubricants
Track pads are undamaged and drive chain are tensioned
Drive sockets and drive chain tumblers are serviceable
Top and bottom swing rollers and tracks are in good overall condition (Should be well lubricated)
Track idlers are serviceable
Back boom stops are functioning
Back A-Frame is in good condition
Boom cut-out (at maximum angle) is operable
Power low lowering is operational
Boom hoist safety auxiliary ratchet / pawl lock is engaging
Boom chords and lacings are undamaged (bent)
Sheave grooves are free from surface defects that could cause rope damage.
Pendant ropers are showing no broken wires (see no. 48)
Operation of steering brakes is satisfactory
Main machinery guarding is in place
Main clutch is fully operational
Main boom and hoist drum brakes are fully functional
